rationale.R1.1: The broad request invites assessment of the paper's thesis, contribution framing, and conceptual positioning of MolPanel.
rationale.R1.2: A meaningful response would be expected to judge whether the argument from ensemble scoring to improved generative filtering is internally coherent.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact foregrounds empirical enrichment and ablation claims, so checking whether those reported results support the claims is central.
rationale.R1.4: Because the piece presents MolPanel as a contribution, a general opinion would normally include whether the idea appears distinct from nearby ensemble or virtual-screening work.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection should preserve uncertainty because the delegation is broad and the artifact is only an excerpt-like draft.
rationale.RX.2: The response responsibility includes avoiding stronger judgments than the provided text can justify.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projecting responsibilities rather than answering or reviewing the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: The artifact concerns drug discovery, so avoiding advice that could encourage unsupported biomedical deployment is always active.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to explicit features of the delegation and artifact.
